bead.title: store: walk-up dead-intermediate cleanup on child close
bead.description:
PROBLEM
A bead with execution-eligible==false and open children blocks its epic from progressing. ddx-e1a576a7 ("bead lifecycle hooks parent: decomposed, not directly execution-eligible", under ddx-a9d130d0) is the canonical example. ddx work never executes it (execution-eligible==false), nothing closes it automatically, and it keeps its parent epic stuck. This is RC-3 in the FEAT-010 reason taxonomy.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/bead/store.go Close() does not walk up the parent chain after closing a child. When a child closes such that the parent's children are all closed (or auto-remediation has resolved them), the parent's execution-eligible==false marker should not block the parent from being re-evaluated as a closure candidate.

PROPOSED FIX
Extend cli/internal/bead/store.go Close(X) — building on the RC-2 supersession cascade (parent bead ddx-04e89823) so the cascades land in one coherent Close() flow. After closing X (and after any supersession cascade triggered by X's close), walk up to X's parent P:
- If P.execution-eligible == false AND len(openChildren(P)) == 0 AND len(totalChildren(P)) > 0, route P into the existing closure-candidate path (the same code path RC-1's classifier fix now triggers for openChildCount==0 epics).
- If P.execution-eligible == false AND P has open children pending their own auto-remediation, do nothing here — the children's own remediations will trigger another walk-up when they close.
- Otherwise do nothing.

Single-hop per Close() invocation (do not chase X's grandparent in the same call). The next Close() in the same loop iteration handles the next level up. Cycle-guarded via the same visited set used by RC-2.

NON-SCOPE
- Auto-closing arbitrary execution-eligible==false beads. Only beads matching the closure-candidate criteria (all children closed, parent is epic-like container) qualify.
- Wiring at the work loop. This is purely Close()-side cleanup.

PARENT AND DEPS
Parent: ddx-2a1ece2b. Depends on ddx-04e89823 (RC-2 supersession cascade, same Close() flow).
bead.acceptance:
1. cli/internal/bead/store.go Close() walks up one hop to the closed bead's parent and routes it into the closure-candidate path if execution-eligible==false AND openChildren(parent)==0 AND totalChildren(parent)>0.
2. TestClose_WalkUp_DeadIntermediateClosureCandidate: parent P (execution-eligible==false, one open child X), close X, assert P is routed to closure candidate.
3. TestClose_WalkUp_DeadIntermediateWithRemainingOpenChildren: parent P (execution-eligible==false, two open children X and Z), close X, assert P remains open (Z still pending).
4. TestClose_WalkUp_SingleHopOnly: chain P → Q → X all execution-eligible==false; close X; assert Q is re-evaluated, P is not (next Close() handles P).
5. The walk-up shares the RC-2 cycle-guard visited set within one Close() call.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... -count=1 green.
7. cd cli && go test ./... green.
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
